adapter_fields.finding: The source context is real but already includes the fixed platform.
adapter_fields.value: fixed microscopic atom/qubit platform, geometry, drive, and measurement context

adapter_fields.finding: The admissibility predicate is supplied by the effective description or experimental protocol, not generated by the source.
The candidate has a concrete before/after effective-sector trace.
The witness is a preserved observation, not yet a source-law witness.
adapter_fields.value: string-breaking, gauge-invariance, edge-mode, or sector-marker record

adapter_fields.finding: The successor context is a projected or constrained view of the same fixed platform rather than a source-owned new algebra.
adapter_fields.value: same microscopic source viewed through an effective sector

adapter_fields.finding: Record preservation is available and is not the blocking field.
adapter_fields.value: experimental and model-comparison record of the sector marker

exact_missing_source_object.also_required: An internal anti-after-naming principle plus W4 perturbation non-factorization under the same preserved record trace.
exact_missing_source_object.required: A native source law that creates a new observable, instrument, or gauge-sector algebra not representable as an effective projection, low-energy restriction, control schedule, boundary condition, access change, or relabeling of a fixed microscopic platform.

fixed_source_rival.reason: The observed sector is reproduced as an effective, low-energy, measurement-shaped, or gauge-language view of a fixed microscopic system with preserved records.

next_required_test: another non-overlapping physical candidate with a source-owned transition law, or a proof that no effective-gauge candidate can supply the missing source-owned algebra object

witness_gates.finding: Effective algebra or sector language appears, but it is not shown to be non-isomorphic source algebra growth rather than a low-energy or projected description of fixed microscopic degrees.
The predicate is defined by the protocol, energy window, measurement pattern, or modeler's gauge language.
The construction space is a prepared platform plus control schedule, not a source-generated new construction grammar.
Perturbations still factor through fixed platform dynamics, low-energy restriction, measurement/dissipation schedule, or coarse-graining.
The sector markers and before/after records can be preserved.
Gauge, access, effective-description, and relabeling absorption are not defeated.
